title: search_duckduckgo uses fixed 500ms sleep as page-load synchronization
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/web.rs:475-583 (search_duckduckgo), line 501
## Violation
After `wait_until_navigated()`, the function calls `std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500))` to wait for the page. This is a race condition: on slow machines the page may not be ready; on fast loads the 500ms is wasted latency. Inside a tokio runtime this also blocks the executor thread because the function is `pub fn`, not async, and is invoked via `block_in_place(handle.block_on(...))` from `execute_web_search`.

```rust
// Wait for page to load
std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500));
```

A similar 2-second blocking sleep exists in `fetch_with_browser` at line 622.
## Mandated refactor
Use a polling wait with timeout: `tab.wait_for_element_with_custom_timeout(".result", Duration::from_secs(5))`. For `fetch_with_browser`, wait for `document.readyState === 'complete'` via `tab.evaluate(...)` polled with a timeout. If a true wall-clock delay is needed, use `tokio::time::sleep` from an async context.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
